title: Genomic DNA Extraction
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ejo1e","text":"1. Don't place any vials containing SDS into an ice tray as the SDS will precipitate ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h3hd","text":"2. Use a laminar flow hood while working with any cultures in autoclaved reagents or cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c7i5v","text":"3. Don't vortex any tubes  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mgg3","text":"4. Dab with a paper towel before putting any new sample on NanoDrop ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajlf","text":"5. Look for a peak at 260 nm, and 260/280 and 260/230 ratios over ~1.8 to confirm a pure gDNA extract","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58p7i","text":"6. NanoDrop may provide an overestimate of DNA concentration (due to the presence of other contaminants like RNA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9h9vd","text":"1. Use gloves while handling Phenol-Chloroform","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3fpa9","text":"2. Do not leave Phenol-Chloroform flask open unnecessarily","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"793tv","text":"3. Keep Phenol-Chloroform flask covered completely with aluminium foil, and store in a dark place","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1e77u","text":"1. Cell Pellet","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"798fv","text":"2. MilliQ Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qoip","text":"3. SDS (10%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7511","text":"4. Proteinase K (20 mg/mL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5stuu","text":"5. RNase A (10 mg/mL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"acd3g","text":"6. Phenol-Chloroform (1:1)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5vii","text":"7. Isopropanol (100%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8u21m","text":"8. Chilled 70% Ethanol ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33hhf","text":"9. Sodium Acetate (3M, pH=5.2)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bi5fu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"664tb","text":"Phenol-Chloroform mixture contains some amount of Isoamyl Alcohol which allows the phase separation to appear distinct","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare cell pellet by centrifuging an overnight culture of E. coli at 4 °C and 7000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00
Gently resuspend the cell pellet in 500 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water by mixing with a pipette and transfer into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (MCT) in a laminar flow hood.
Add 75 µL of SDS and 3 µL of proteinase K (or how much ever volume is needed to achieve a final concentration of 100 ug/mL).
Heat at 95 °C for 00:05:00 to inactivate proteinase K.
Let the tube cool to Room temperature Add 5 µL of RNase A (or how much ever volume is needed to achieve a final concentration of 200 ug/mL). Leave the tube at room temperature for 00:10:00 .

Add 1 mL of Phenol-Chloroform mixture to the tube. Centrifuge at 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C The contents of the tube should phase separate into three layers: an aqueous layer on top, a viscous jelly-like layer in the middle, and a layer of chloroform at the bottom.

Collect the aqueous layer and the jelly-like layer using a cut tube and transfer them into a fresh MCT. Add 1 mL of Phenol-Chloroform mixture to this tube. Centrifuge again at 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C The contents of the tube should phase separate into three layers as before.
Collect only the aqueous layer at the top and transfer it into a fresh MCT. Add 160 µL of 3 Molarity (M) Sodium Acetate to this tube. Mix gently.
Add 1 mL of Isopropanol to the tube and mix gently by inversion till white strands of DNA precipitate out Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C
Discard the supernatant. Add 1 mL of Chilled 70% Ethanol gently along the walls of the tube, without disturbing the DNA pellet. Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C
Air-dry the tube till there isn't any ethanol remaining.
Resuspend the DNA pellet gently in 100 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water by mixing with a pipette under a laminar flow hood. Store the suspension in a -30 °C refrigerator.
Load 1 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water as blank on NanoDrop and load 1 µL of gDNA suspension as the sample to measure its concentration.
Prepare a 1% agarose gel by adding 0.5 g of agarose in 50 mL of TAE buffer with 2 µL of ethidium bromide.
Load 3 µL of 1kb DNA Ladder into the first lane. Load 1 µL gDNA + 1 µL dye into the second lane. Run the gel. Observe for a single band above the first band of the ladder, close to the well.
creator: Likhith Chandragiri
